section-1.title: Contact between prescribed persons and adopted person's relatives
section-1.content: (1)In section 98 of the Adoption and Children Act 2002 (pre-commencement adoptions: information), after subsection (1) insert—/nTEXT REMOVED. MUST NOT BE ANNOTATED/n(2)In each of subsections (2) and (3) of that section, for “that purpose” substitute“ a purpose within subsection (1) or (1A) ”./n(3)In subsection (7) of that section, after the definition of “appointed day” insert—/nTEXT REMOVED. MUST NOT BE ANNOTATED

section-2.title: Placement of looked after children with prospective adopters
section-2.content: (1)Section 22C of the Children Act 1989 is amended as follows./n(2)In subsection (7), after “subject to” insert“ subsection (9B) and ”./n(3)After subsection (9) insert—/nTEXT REMOVED. MUST NOT BE ANNOTATED

section-3.title: Repeal of requirement to give due consideration to ethnicity: England
section-3.content: (1)Section 1 of the Adoption and Children Act 2002 (considerations applying when making decisions about the adoption of a child) is amended as follows./n(2)In subsection (5) (due consideration to be given to religious persuasion, racial origin and cultural and linguistic background), for “In placing the child for adoption, the adoption agency” substitute“ In placing a child for adoption, an adoption agency in Wales ”./n(3)In consequence of the amendment made by subsection (2)—/n(a)in subsection (1), for “This section applies” substitute“ Subsections (2) to (4) apply ”;/n(b)in subsection (6), for “The court or adoption agency” substitute“ In coming to a decision relating to the adoption of a child, a court or adoption agency ”;/n(c)after subsection (8) insert—/nTEXT REMOVED. MUST NOT BE ANNOTATED

section-4.title: Recruitment, assessment and approval of prospective adopters

section-5.title: Adoption support services: personal budgets
section-5.content: In Chapter 2 of Part 1 of the Adoption and Children Act 2002 (the Adoption Service) after section 4 insert—

section-6.title: Adoption support services: duty to provide information
section-6.content: In Chapter 2 of Part 1 of the Adoption and Children Act 2002 (the Adoption Service) after section 4A (as inserted by section 5) insert—

section-7.title: The Adoption and Children Act Register
section-7.content: (1)The Adoption and Children Act 2002 is amended as follows./n(2)In section 125 (Adoption and Children Act Register)—/n(a)in subsection (1)(a), after “children who are suitable for adoption” insert“ , children for whom a local authority in England are considering adoption ”;/n(b)in subsection (3), after “search” insert“ (subject to regulations under section 128A) ”./n(3)In section 128 (supply of information for the register), in subsection (4)(b), after “children suitable for adoption” insert“ or for whom a local authority in England are considering adoption ”./n(4)After section 128 insert—

section-8.title: Contact: children in care of local authorities
section-8.content: (1)Section 34 of the Children Act 1989 (parental contact etc with children in care) is amended as follows./n(2)In subsection (1), after “subject to the provisions of this section” insert“ and their duty under section 22(3)(a) ”./n(3)After subsection (6) insert—/nTEXT REMOVED. MUST NOT BE ANNOTATED/n(4)In subsection (8), before paragraph (a) insert—/nTEXT REMOVED. MUST NOT BE ANNOTATED/n(5)In subsection (11) after “Before” insert“ making, varying or discharging an order under this section or ”.

section-9.title: Contact: post-adoption
section-9.content: (1)After section 51 of the Adoption and Children Act 2002 insert—

section-10.title: Family mediation information and assessment meetings
section-10.content: (1)Before making a relevant family application, a person must attend a family mediation information and assessment meeting./n(2)Family Procedure Rules—/n(a)may provide for subsection (1) not to apply in circumstances specified in the Rules,/n(b)may make provision about convening a family mediation information and assessment meeting, or about the conduct of such a meeting,/n(c)may make provision for the court not to issue, or otherwise deal with, an application if, in contravention of subsection (1), the applicant has not attended a family mediation information and assessment meeting, and/n(d)may provide for a determination as to whether an applicant has contravened subsection (1) to be made after considering only evidence of a description specified in the Rules./n(3)In this section—/n(4)This section is without prejudice to sections 75 and 76 of the Courts Act 2003 (power to make Family Procedure Rules).

section-11.title: Welfare of the child: parental involvement
section-11.content: (1)Section 1 of the Children Act 1989 (welfare of the child) is amended as follows./n(2)After subsection (2) insert—/nTEXT REMOVED. MUST NOT BE ANNOTATED/n(3)After subsection (5) insert—/nTEXT REMOVED. MUST NOT BE ANNOTATED

section-12.title: Child arrangements orders
section-12.content: (1)Section 8(1) of the Children Act 1989 is amended as follows./n(2)Omit the definitions of “contact order” and “residence order”./n(3)After “In this Act—” insert—/nTEXT REMOVED. MUST NOT BE ANNOTATED/n(4)Schedule 2 (amendments relating to child arrangements orders) has effect.

section-13.title: Control of expert evidence, and of assessments, in children proceedings
section-13.content: (1)A person may not without the permission of the court instruct a person to provide expert evidence for use in children proceedings./n(2)Where in contravention of subsection (1) a person is instructed to provide expert evidence, evidence resulting from the instructions is inadmissible in children proceedings unless the court rules that it is admissible./n(3)A person may not without the permission of the court cause a child to be medically or psychiatrically examined or otherwise assessed for the purposes of the provision of expert evidence in children proceedings./n(4)Where in contravention of subsection (3) a child is medically or psychiatrically examined or otherwise assessed, evidence resulting from the examination or other assessment is inadmissible in children proceedings unless the court rules that it is admissible./n(5)In children proceedings, a person may not without the permission of the court put expert evidence (in any form) before the court./n(6)The court may give permission as mentioned in subsection (1), (3) or (5) only if the court is of the opinion that the expert evidence is necessary to assist the court to resolve the proceedings justly./n(7)When deciding whether to give permission as mentioned in subsection (1), (3) or (5) the court is to have regard in particular to—/n(a)any impact which giving permission would be likely to have on the welfare of the children concerned, including in the case of permission as mentioned in subsection (3) any impact which any examination or other assessment would be likely to have on the welfare of the child who would be examined or otherwise assessed,/n(b)the issues to which the expert evidence would relate,/n(c)the questions which the court would require the expert to answer,/n(d)what other expert evidence is available (whether obtained before or after the start of proceedings),/n(e)whether evidence could be given by another person on the matters on which the expert would give evidence,/n(f)the impact which giving permission would be likely to have on the timetable for, and duration and conduct of, the proceedings,/n(g)the cost of the expert evidence, and/n(h)any matters prescribed by Family Procedure Rules./n(8)References in this section to providing expert evidence, or to putting expert evidence before a court, do not include references to—/n(a)the provision or giving of evidence—/n(i)by a person who is a member of the staff of a local authority or of an authorised applicant,/n(ii)in proceedings to which the authority or authorised applicant is a party, and/n(iii)in the course of the person's work for the authority or authorised applicant,/n(b)the provision or giving of evidence—/n(i)by a person within a description prescribed for the purposes of subsection (1) of section 94 of the Adoption and Children Act 2002 (suitability for adoptionetc.), and/n(ii)about the matters mentioned in that subsection,/n(c)the provision or giving of evidence by an officer of the Children and Family Court Advisory and Support Service when acting in that capacity, or/n(d)the provision or giving of evidence by a Welsh family proceedings officer (as defined by section 35(4) of the Children Act 2004) when acting in that capacity./n(9)In this section—/n(10)The preceding provisions of this section are without prejudice to sections 75 and 76 of the Courts Act 2003 (power to make Family Procedure Rules)./n(11)In section 38 of the Children Act 1989 (court's power to make interim care and supervision orders, and to give directions as to medical examination etc. of children) after subsection (7) insert—/nTEXT REMOVED. MUST NOT BE ANNOTATED

section-14.title: Care, supervision and other family proceedings: time limits and timetables
section-14.content: (1)The Children Act 1989 is amended as follows./n(2)In section 32(1)(a) (timetable for dealing with application for care or supervision order) for “disposing of the application without delay; and” substitute“disposing of the application—/nTEXT REMOVED. MUST NOT BE ANNOTATED/n(3)In section 32 (care and supervision orders) after subsection (2) insert—/nTEXT REMOVED. MUST NOT BE ANNOTATED/n(4)In section 38 (interim care and supervision orders)—/n(a)in subsection (4) (duration of interim order) omit—/n(i)paragraph (a) (order may not last longer than 8 weeks), and/n(ii)paragraph (b) (subsequent order generally may not last longer than 4 weeks),/n(b)in that subsection after paragraph (d) insert—/nTEXT REMOVED. MUST NOT BE ANNOTATED/nthe expiry of the period of eight weeks beginning with the date on which the order is made;”, and/n(c)omit subsection (5) (interpretation of subsection (4)(b))./n(5)In section 11(1) (section 8 orders: court's duty, in the light of rules made by virtue of section 11(2), to draw up timetable and give directions to implement it) for “rules made by virtue of subsection (2))” substitute“ provision in rules of court that is of the kind mentioned in subsection (2)(a) or (b)) ”./n(6)In section 14E(1) (special guardianship orders: court's duty, in the light of rules made by virtue of subsection (3), to draw up timetable and give directions to implement it) for “rules made by virtue of subsection (3))” substitute“ provision in rules of court that is of the kind mentioned in section 11(2)(a) or (b)) ”./n(7)In section 32(1) (care and supervision orders: court's duty, in the light of rules made by virtue of section 32(2), to draw up timetable and give directions to implement it)—/n(a)for “hearing an application for an order under this Part” substitute“ in which an application for an order under this Part is proceeding ”, and/n(b)for “rules made by virtue of subsection (2))” substitute“ provision in rules of court that is of the kind mentioned in subsection (2)(a) or (b)) ”./n(8)In section 109(1) of the Adoption and Children Act 2002 (adoption and placement orders: court's duty, in the light of rules made by virtue of section 109(2), to draw up timetable and give directions to implement it) for “rules made by virtue of subsection (2))” substitute“ provision in rules of court that is of the kind mentioned in subsection (2)(a) or (b)) ”.

section-15.title: Care plans
section-15.content: (1)For section 31(3A) of the Children Act 1989 (no care order to be made until court has considered section 31A care plan) substitute—/nTEXT REMOVED. MUST NOT BE ANNOTATED/n(2)In section 31A of the Children Act 1989 (care plans)—/n(a)in subsection (1) (where application made for care order, care plan to be prepared within such time as the court may direct) for “the court may direct” substitute“ may be prescribed ”, and/n(b)after subsection (4) insert—/nTEXT REMOVED. MUST NOT BE ANNOTATED/n(3)In consequence of subsection (1), section 121(1) of the Adoption and Children Act 2002 is repealed.

section-16.title: Care proceedings and care plans: regulations: procedural requirements
section-16.content: (1)In section 104 of the Children Act 1989 (regulations and orders)—/n(a)in subsections (2) and (3A) (regulations within subsection (3B) or (3C) not subject to annulment but to be approved in draft) after “(3B)” insert“ , (3BA) ”, and/n(b)after subsection (3B) insert—/nTEXT REMOVED. MUST NOT BE ANNOTATED/n(2). . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .

section-17.title: Repeal of restrictions on divorce and dissolution etc where there are children
section-17.content: (1)The following are repealed—/n(a)section 41 of the Matrimonial Causes Act 1973 (in proceedings for divorce etc. court is to consider whether to exercise powers under Children Act 1989);/n(b)section 63 of the Civil Partnership Act 2004 (in proceedings for dissolution etc. court is to consider whether to exercise powers under Children Act 1989)./n(2)The following amendments and repeals are in consequence of the repeals made by subsection (1)./n(3)In section 9(1)(a) of the Matrimonial Causes Act 1973 (proceedings after decree of divorce: power to make decree absolute is subject to section 41)—/n(a)for “sections” substitute“ section ”, and/n(b)omit “and 41”./n(4)In section 17(2) of that Act (grant of decree of judicial separation is subject to section 41) omit “, subject to section 41 below,”./n(5)Omit paragraph 31 of Schedule 12 to the Children Act 1989 (which substitutes section 41 of the Matrimonial Causes Act 1973)./n(6)In section 40(4)(b) of the Civil Partnership Act 2004 (proceedings after conditional order: power to make order final is subject to section 63) omit the words from “and section 63” to the end./n(7)In section 56(3) of that Act (making of separation order is subject to section 63) omit “, subject to section 63,”.

section-18.title: Repeal of uncommenced provisions of Part 2 of the Family Law Act 1996
section-18.content: (1)Part 2 of the Family Law Act 1996 (divorce and separation), except section 22 (the only provision of Part 2 which is in force), is repealed./n(2)In consequence of subsection (1), the following provisions of the Family Law Act 1996 (which relate to provisions of Part 2) are repealed—/n(a)section 1(c) and (d),/n(b)section 63(2)(a),/n(c)section 64(1)(a),/n(d)in section 65(5) the words “to rules made under section 12 or”,/n(e)Part 1 of Schedule 8, except—/n(i)paragraph 16(5)(a), (6)(b) and (7) (which have been brought into force), and/n(ii)paragraphs 4 and 16(1) (which relate to those provisions),/n(f)in Schedule 9, paragraphs 1 and 2 and, in paragraph 4, the definitions of “decree”, “instrument” and “petition”, and/n(j)in Schedule 10, the entries relating to—/n(i)the Matrimonial Causes Act 1973,/n(ii)the Domicile and Matrimonial Proceedings Act 1973,/n(iii)sections 1, 7 and 63 of, and paragraph 38 of Schedule 2 to, the Domestic Proceedings and Magistrates' Courts Act 1978,/n(iv)the Senior Courts Act 1981,/n(v)the Administration of Justice Act 1982,/n(vi)the Matrimonial and Family Proceedings Act 1984,/n(vii)the Family Law Act 1986, and/n(viii)Schedule 13 to the Children Act 1989./n(3)In consequence of subsections (1) and (2), the following provisions are repealed—/n(a)paragraphs 50 to 52 of Schedule 4 to the Access to Justice Act 1999,/n(b)the following provisions of the Welfare Reform and Pensions Act 1999—/n(i)section 28(1)(b) and (c), (2), (4) and (5),/n(ii)section 48(1)(b) and (c), (2), (4) and (5), and/n(iii)in Schedule 12, paragraphs 64 to 66,/n(c)paragraphs 22 to 25 of Schedule 1 to the Constitutional Reform Act 2005,/n(d)paragraph 12 of Schedule 2 to the Children and Adoption Act 2006, and/n(e)the following provisions of Schedule 5 to the Legal Aid, Sentencing and Punishment of Offenders Act 2012—/n(i)paragraphs 43 to 45, and/n(ii)in the second column of the Table in Part 2, paragraph (l) of the entry relating to Schedule 4 to the Access to Justice Act 1999./n(4)In consequence of subsection (1), in section 1 of the Family Law Act 1996 (general principles underlying Part 2), in the words before paragraph (a) and in the title, for “Parts II and III” substitute“ section 22 ”./n(5)In consequence of subsection (3)(b)(i), in section 28(11) of the Welfare Reform and Pensions Act 1999 (interpretation of subsections (4)(b), (5)(c) and (6)) for “subsections (4)(b), (5)(c) and” substitute“ subsection ”./n(6)The modifications set out in subsection (7), which were originally made by article 3(2) of the No. 2 Order and article 4 of the No. 3 Order, are to continue to have effect but as amendments of the provisions concerned (rather than as modifications having effect until the coming into force of provisions of the Family Law Act 1996 repealed by this section without having come into force)./n(7)The modifications are—/n(a)in section 22(2) of the Matrimonial and Family Proceedings Act 1984 for the words from “if” to “granted” substitute“ if a decree of divorce, a decree of nullity of marriage or a decree of judicial separation has been granted ”, and/n(b)in section 31 of the Matrimonial Causes Act 1973—/n(i)in subsection (7D) for “Subsections (7) and (8) of section 22A” substitute“ Section 23(6) ”,/n(ii)in subsection (7D) for “section 22A” substitute“ section 23 ”, and/n(iii)in subsection (7F) for “section 23A” substitute“ section 24 ”./n(8)In section 31(7D) of the Matrimonial Causes Act 1973—/n(a)for “apply”, in the first place, substitute“ applies ”, and/n(b)for “they apply where it” substitute“ it applies where the court ”./n(9)Articles 3(2) and 4 of the No. 2 Order, and article 4 of the No. 3 Order, are revoked; and in subsection (6) and this subsection—
